old_claim.note: Digital Signal Processing (DSP) provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because the node is scoped to first-generation commercial DWDM optical transport, before coherent-DSP optical systems became the defining architecture.

source_shape.source_locator: IEEE Communications Society timeline item identifies the first commercial 16-channel DWDM system deployed for Sprint in 1996 without making digital signal processing part of the defining deployment claim.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: First commercial DWDM is a wavelength-multiplexed optical transport deployment, not a coherent-DSP architecture.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports removing DSP as a direct prerequisite for the 1996 commercial DWDM scope; DSP may belong on later coherent optical transport nodes.

ontology_before: The graph made first-generation DWDM depend directly on digital signal processing by broad inference.
ontology_after: The graph keeps DWDM tied to fiber optics and lasers, leaving DSP for later coherent optical networking where it is technically central.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: direct dense_wavelength_division_multiplexing -> digital_signal_processing edge removed.
would_reject_if: The node were rescoped to coherent optical DWDM transponders rather than first commercial DWDM optical transport.
A stronger source showed the 1996 Sprint/Ciena deployment required DSP as a defining technology.
short_reason: DSP is not a direct prerequisite for first-generation commercial DWDM.
